content:
✨ 1Hosts | HTML
🎯 Primary Use Case: To provide users with comprehensive DNS blocklists that can be easily integrated into their existing network or device configurations to block unwanted content like ads, trackers, and malware.
✨ Key Features: • Multiple blocklist variants (Lite, Xtra) • Support for various clients and formats (AdAway, Pi-hole, dnscrypt-proxy, uBlock Origin, AdGuardHome, dnsmasq, Unbound, Windows hosts file, Little Snitch)
📖 Summary: 1Hosts provides curated DNS blocklists designed to enhance privacy, security, and user experience by filtering out ads, trackers, and malware. It offers different variants and formats compatible with various DNS filtering software and operating systems.
🔗 Links: • View Project • Homepage ================ 🔓 Open Source

💡 linux-android | Shell
🎯 Primary Use Case: Repurposing old Android phones into Linux desktops or smart home servers.
✨ Key Features: • Install Linux desktop environments (XFCE4, LXQt, MATE, KDE Plasma) on Android via Termux • Set up Home Assistant for smart home control • No root or PC required • Supports GPU acceleration on Qualcomm Snapdragon chips • Provides scripts to start and stop the Linux environment
📖 Summary: This repository provides shell scripts to install and run a full Linux desktop environment or a Home Assistant smart home server on an Android device using Termux. It allows repurposing old Android phones without requiring root access or a PC.
🔗 Links: • View Project • Homepage ================ 🔓 Open Source

🔥 Open-LLM-VTuber | Python
🎯 Primary Use Case: To create a personal, interactive AI companion with a visual avatar that can be controlled and conversed with using natural voice commands, running locally for privacy and accessibility.
✨ Key Features: • Hands-free voice interaction • Voice interruption • Live2D avatar integration • Local execution (offline support) • Cross-platform compatibility
📖 Summary: Open-LLM-VTuber is a Python-based project enabling hands-free, voice-interactive AI companions with Live2D avatars. It supports real-time conversations, voice interruption, and can run entirely offline on local machines across different platforms.
🔗 Links: • View Project • Homepage ================ 🔓 Open Source

🚀 babysitter | JavaScript
🎯 Primary Use Case: To provide a framework for building and managing reliable, auditable, and controlled AI agent workflows, ensuring they execute tasks precisely as defined and preventing unintended or erroneous actions.
✨ Key Features: • Enforces obedience on agentic workforces • Manages complex tasks and workflows • Deterministic, hallucination-free self-orchestration
📖 Summary: Babysitter is a JavaScript library designed to enforce strict obedience and deterministic execution within AI agent workforces. It enables the management of complex tasks and workflows by ensuring agents follow predefined steps, pass quality gates, and undergo human approval when necessary, all while maintaining an immutable record of decisions.
🔗 Links: • View Project • Homepage ================ 🔓 Open Source

✨ hermes-webui | Python
🎯 Primary Use Case: To provide a convenient and accessible web-based interface for interacting with the Hermes Agent, mirroring its full command-line functionality.
✨ Key Features: • Web-based interface for Hermes Agent • Full CLI parity • Three-panel layout (sidebar, chat, workspace) • Composer footer for controls • Circular context ring for token usage
📖 Summary: Hermes WebUI provides a lightweight, dark-themed web interface for the Hermes Agent, offering full parity with its CLI experience. It allows users to interact with the autonomous agent through a browser, managing sessions, workspaces, and configurations with a user-friendly three-panel layout.
🔗 Links: • View Project • Homepage ================ 🔓 Open Source
